fed_stance.note: FedWatch direct probabilities were not accessible in-session, but the latest repository-verified same-week read remained overwhelmingly centered on no change for the April 28-29 FOMC meeting.

real_yields.note: FRED DFII10 rose from 1.89 on 2026-04-14 to 1.90 on 2026-04-15, a 1 bp change that remains inside the flat band.

dxy.note: DXY was down on the day, down over five days, and down over one month on the TradingView page, keeping the broader dollar signal weak.

risk_mood.note: VIX remained in the 15-20 balanced band.

vix_direction.note: Spot VIX was lower than the prior close, a mild tailwind for equities.

growth.note: GDPNow remains at 1.3% for 2026:Q1, which is soft but not a fresh re-acceleration or collapse.

credit_spreads.note: HY OAS widened by 1 bp from 2.84 on 2026-04-14 to 2.85 on 2026-04-15.

curve.note: The 2s10s curve steepened modestly from 0.53 to 0.54.

move.note: Rates volatility continued to ease on both the daily and five-day view.

sox.note: Semiconductors remained decisively higher on both the one-day and five-day horizons.

oil_supply.note: The current EIA weekly package and ongoing Gulf supply-risk backdrop still point to a tightening supply impulse rather than easing physical balances.

inventories.note: The latest repository-verified weekly petroleum balance remained a crude build, which limits crude upside conviction.

geopolitical.note: Same-day market coverage emphasized Iran-deal optimism and lower oil prices, indicating that the immediate war premium is easing even though regional risk remains present.

gold_etf_flows.note: The World Gold Council page confirmed the latest update is ETF Flows: March 2026, and the repository-verified March reading remains one of heavy outflows.

ecb_stance.note: The ECB kept rates unchanged on 19 March 2026 while emphasizing upside inflation risks and no pre-commitment to a rate path.

eurozone_growth.note: No fresh same-session euro-area growth impulse was verified in this run, so the eurozone growth input is kept neutral for conservatism.

rba_stance.note: Recent official RBA communication said the Bank had to tighten policy in response to inflation that remains too high.

aud_rate_diff.note: The RBA at 4.10% versus a neutral Fed backdrop keeps the AUD-USD rate differential supportive.

china_growth.note: China manufacturing PMI stayed above 50 but slowed materially from February to March.

copper.note: Copper was lower on the latest session on the allowed fallback source.

boj_stance.note: The BoJ kept the overnight call rate at 0.75% on 19 March 2026, but the regime remains one of normalization rather than ultra-easy policy.

jpy_rate_diff.note: BoJ normalization versus a neutral Fed continues to support the JPY side of the rate differential.

eur_rate_diff.note: A firm ECB hold against a neutral Fed leaves the EUR-USD rate backdrop supportive for the euro rather than hostile.

6E.analysis: The euro remains bullish. The ECB's official March decision read as a firm hold with explicit upside inflation risks, and that combines with a weaker dollar to support EUR futures. Because euro-area growth was not freshly re-accelerating in this run, conviction is solid but not extreme.

6A.analysis: The Australian dollar is neutral in this refresh. A hawkish RBA and supportive rate differential are being offset by slower China PMI momentum and a lower copper session. Without a clearer pro-growth impulse from China or commodities, 6A loses the stronger upside alignment seen in earlier runs.
